images.caption: Figure 1:Teaser Demonstration.We propose a novel memory mechanism for video world models through learning-based adaptive context querying. Compared to prior rule-based context retrieval methods[cam,worldmem,vmem], our approach handles scenes with occlusions and dynamic objects. This figure highlights dynamic objects, representative occluders, video generation trajectory, and key frames.

Figure 2:Architecture clarification.(a) Interaction mechanism among𝐂\mathbf{C},𝐐\mathbf{Q}, and𝐏\mathbf{P}tokens; (b)&(c) Two designs for context querying, where the alternative design in (b) fails in experiments (Sec.5.4), and the adopted design in (c) leverages the prior knowledge of the video generation model itself and performs effectively.

Figure 3:Model Architecture.Our video generation model adopts a Diffusion Transformer. We concatenate𝐂\mathbf{C},𝐐\mathbf{Q},𝐏\mathbf{P}tokens for context-conditioned long video generation. An optional camera encoder supports interactive control.

Figure 4:Efficiency Strategies.(a) Strategy#1: Context querying in shallow Query Layers with𝐂\mathbf{C},𝐐\mathbf{Q},𝐏\mathbf{P}tokens; deep Generative Layers use only𝐐\mathbf{Q},𝐏\mathbf{P}tokens. (b) Strategy#2: Remove unnecessary attention computation for improved efficiency.
Figure 5:Qualitative Results.MemLearnereffectively handles both indoor (a, b) and outdoor (c, Fig.1) scenes with occlusions and dynamic objects.
Figure 6:Real-World Qualitative Results.By incorporating real-world videos into training dataset,MemLearnergeneralizes to real-world scenes.

Figure 7:Qualitative Comparison.MemLearnerachieves optimal memory and visual quality, demonstrating learning-based adaptive context query effectiveness. Other methods show inconsistent memory performance.
Table 3:Quantitative comparison on CaM dataset[cam](no occlusions/dynamics). Small performance gaps, widening sharply on our occlusion/dynamic dataset (Tab.2).
Figure 8:Attention computation settings for ablation study in Tab.5. Performance impact of attention scope whenQtokens act as queries.
Figure 9:Overview of the base text-to-video generation model.
Figure 12:Additional attention computation settings. Horizontal tokens serve as queries; vertical tokens serve as keys/values.
Figure 13:Additional Dataset Ablation Results. “Rendered” denotes results trained only on rendered videos, while “+Real” indicates results after adding real-world videos to the training set.
